target.title: fix(ui): add agent selector to dreaming tab
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: the Control UI Dreaming tab had no agent selector, so users could not switch Dreaming context between agent workspaces.
- Why it matters: Dreaming artifacts (`DREAMS.md`, short-term recall, schedules, memory state) are agent-specific, so the tab was effectively stuck to the current session context.
- What changed: added an agent selector to the Dreaming tab chrome and wired it to the same preferred-session-per-agent logic used by chat session controls.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no backend dreaming behavior, routing behavior, or storage behavior was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63558
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming tab lacked an agent selector, preventing users from switching agent contexts there.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts > dreaming view > renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)
```
- Observed result after fix: the Dreaming tab now renders an agent selector when multiple agents are available, preselects the active agent, and triggers agent-context switching through the same preferred-session resolution used elsewhere.
- What was not tested: end-to-end browser interaction against a live multi-agent gateway.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dreaming used its own tab chrome and never reused the shared agent-selection/session-switching logic used in chat.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Dreaming view tests did not assert presence of an agent selector for multi-agent setups.
- Contributing context (if known): the rest of Control UI already assumed agent-specific contexts for many views, so Dreaming lagged behind that UX pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when multiple agents are available, Dreaming shows an agent selector, reflects the active agent, and calls back when the selection changes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the missing feature is entirely in the Dreaming tab view/chrome layer.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds `renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts`.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Control UI Dreaming tab now shows an agent selector when multiple agent contexts are available, so users can switch Dreaming context directly from the tab.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI / Dreaming tab

### Steps

1. Render Dreaming with multiple available agent options.
2. Verify an agent selector is present.
3. Verify the current agent is selected.
4. Change the selection and verify the callback fires with the target agent id.

### Expected

- Dreaming shows the current agent and allows agent switching.

### Actual

- Dreaming view test passes with the new selector behavior.
- `tsgo:core` passes.
- `lint:core` passes.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the selector appears in the Dreaming tab chrome.
- Verified the selected agent is reflected in the UI.
- Verified agent changes route through preferred-session-per-agent logic.
- Verified view test, `tsgo:core`, and `lint:core` all pass.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the selector could route users to an unexpected session for an agent.
  - Mitigation: it uses the same preferred-session-per-agent resolution logic already used in chat controls.
- Risk: single-agent setups could get cluttered.
  - Mitigation: the selector renders only when more than one agent option is available.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts (27 tests) 89ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  27 passed (27)
    Start at  00:25:12
    Duration  470ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Selecting an agent in the Dreaming tab called `switchChatSession()` but `loadDreamingStatus` and `loadDreamDiary` still sent `{}` to `doctor.memory.*`, showing the default agent's data regardless of selection. The tab could display DREAMS.md and memory state for the wrong agent.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 27/27 gateway method tests pass. TypeScript `tsc --noEmit` reports no errors across core and extensions configs.
**Observed result after fix:** `onSelectAgent` sets `selectedAgentId` on state, immediately reloads `dreamingStatus` and `dreamDiary` scoped to the selected agent. Gateway handlers accept optional `agentId` param with backward-compatible fallback to `resolveDefaultAgentId` when omitted.
**What was not tested:** Live multi-agent Control UI browser session; gateway handler routing is verified at the unit level through the existing 27 gateway method tests.

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Control UI could restore a historical routed session key like `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` from persisted local settings after reload.
- Why it matters: users would reopen Control UI and land in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI look misrouted even though the backend heartbeat target remained main.
- What changed: session restore now sanitizes persisted routed direct/group session keys during settings load and falls back to `main` instead.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway routing behavior, heartbeat behavior, or live session switching logic was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61661
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI restoring historical routed direct/group sessions from persisted local settings instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm vitest run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed direct session
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       18 passed (18)
```
- Observed result after fix: persisted `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` session selections are normalized back to `main` during settings restore.
- What was not tested: explicit session selection through URL query params or live backend routing changes, because this PR only changes local restore behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Control UI storage restore trusted persisted `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` values without filtering out historical routed direct/group sessions.
- Missing detection / guardrail: storage tests covered scoped session persistence but did not cover stale routed-session restore behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): main-session normalization already existed later in the gateway boot path, but the bad routed session had already won at the storage restore layer.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: persisted routed `direct`/`group` session keys fall back to `main` on Control UI restore.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug originates in local persisted settings load, so the storage tests are the exact boundary where the regression occurs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds two restore-specific cases for routed `direct` and `group` sessions.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

After reload/reopen, Control UI will no longer restore stale routed `direct`/`group` sessions from persisted local settings; it will fall back to the main session instead.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI local storage restore

### Steps

1. Seed persisted Control UI settings with a routed direct/group session key in `sessionsByGateway`.
2. Call `loadSettings()`.
3. Verify restored `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` fall back to `main`.

### Expected

- routed historical sessions should not win restore by default
- restored settings should point back to main

### Actual

- both new routed-session storage tests pass
- `tsgo:core` passes
- `lint:core` passes

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the restore sanitization happens in `ui/src/ui/storage.ts`, which is the correct layer for this bug.
- Verified routed `direct` and `group` session keys now restore to `main` in storage tests.
- Verified core type/lint checks pass on the changed files.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a user might intentionally want to reopen a routed direct/group session from persisted state.
  - Mitigation: this PR only changes the automatic restore default; explicit session selection can still be handled through current UI/navigation flows.
- Risk: over-matching could reset legitimate session keys.
  - Mitigation: the sanitization is narrowly limited to `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` patterns only.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  ui  ../../ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts (22 tests) 38ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  22 passed (22)
    Start at  00:14:43
    Duration  611ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Routed direct/group session keys persisted in localStorage were not cleared on restore. The regex `/^agent:[^:]+:[^:]+:(direct|group):.+$/` only matched 5-segment per-channel-peer keys, missing `agent:<id>:direct:<peer>` (per-peer) and `agent:<id>:<channel>:<acct>:direct:<peer>` (per-account-channel-peer) shapes emitted by `buildAgentPeerSessionKey`.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, jsdom, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 22/22 tests pass including two new tests covering per-peer (`agent:main:direct:ou_123`) and per-account-channel-peer (`agent:main:feishu:acct_abc:direct:ou_123`) shapes, both correctly falling back to `main`.
**Observed result after fix:** All three `buildAgentPeerSessionKey` direct shapes and group shapes reset to main on storage restore.
**What was not tested:** Live Control UI reload in a browser with actual localStorage entries; all three direct key shapes verified via jsdom storage unit tests.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): make wiki_lint tool output path-safe
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `wiki_lint` returned the raw lint result as tool `details`, including an absolute `reportPath`, while the CLI path works normally.
- Why it matters: absolute-path-heavy tool output can interact poorly with tool-result handling and produced `unable to resolve opened file path` in the reported runtime path.
- What changed: the `wiki_lint` tool now renders vault-internal report paths relative to the wiki vault with a defensive formatter, and returns a lightweight details object instead of the raw lint result object.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the underlying `lintMemoryWikiVault()` result shape and CLI behavior are unchanged; CLI callers still receive the absolute report path they can read from disk.

AI-assisted PR: yes. I used OpenClaw/Codex assistance, reviewed the diff, and personally ran the verification below.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83420
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `wiki_lint` tool output should avoid exposing the raw absolute `reportPath` in model-visible text and should not return the raw lint result object as details.
- Real environment tested:
  - Host: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic x64
  - Container: `node:24-bookworm`
  - Source: `origin/main` plus this patch
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v /tmp/openclaw-83420-main:/repo \
  -w /repo node:24-bookworm \
  bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null && pnpm test:extension memory-wiki'
```

I also ran a direct Docker harness invoking `createWikiLintTool(...).execute(...)` against an isolated test vault with lint warnings.

- Evidence after fix (copied live output):

```text
[test-extension] Running 23 test files for memory-wiki
...
Test Files  23 passed (23)
Tests       118 passed (118)
```

Direct current-head tool output after the patch (`69baeae2888b1e652740c3afbdabf08f4ae84eb3`):

```text
toolText=Issues: 3 total (0 errors, 3 warnings)\nContradictions: 0\nOpen questions: 0\nProvenance gaps: 1\nReport: reports/lint.md
detailsReportPath=reports/lint.md
detailsHasVaultRoot=false
detailsContainsVaultRoot=false
toolTextContainsVaultRoot=false
coreLintReportPathAbsolute=true
coreLintReportPathContainsVaultRoot=true
```

- Observed result after fix: the tool-visible summary reports `reports/lint.md`, `details.reportPath` is also `reports/lint.md`, and the tool details no longer include or serialize the vault root. The underlying lint core result still uses an absolute report path for file-oriented callers. If a report path is not inside the vault, the formatter leaves it unchanged instead of fabricating a misleading relative path.
- What was not tested: I did not reproduce the exact full-runtime `unable to resolve opened file path` failure path in Docker; the direct tool harness reproduced the risky output shape and confirmed the patched output shape.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the patch, the same direct harness returned `Report: /tmp/wiki-lint-repro-vault/reports/lint.md` and `details.reportPath: /tmp/wiki-lint-repro-vault/reports/lint.md`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `createWikiLintTool()` reused the raw `lintMemoryWikiVault()` result directly for both visible text and tool metadata. That raw result is appropriate for CLI/file callers but too file-path-heavy for an agent tool result.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no tool-level test ensuring `wiki_lint` normalizes report paths for tool output.
- Contributing context (if known): the CLI already logs a relative report path internally, but the returned result keeps the absolute path for CLI consumers.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `wiki_lint` returns `reports/lint.md` in visible text and `details.reportPath`, without leaking the absolute vault path in the visible summary or details payload, while preserving categorized lint details such as broken wikilinks.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the tool wrapper, not the underlying linter or CLI path.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`wiki_lint` tool responses now show the lint report path relative to the vault (`reports/lint.md`) instead of an absolute local path. CLI lint output is unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
wiki_lint tool -> raw lint result -> absolute reportPath in text/details

After:
wiki_lint tool -> normalized tool payload -> relative reportPath in text/details
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic x64
- Runtime/container: Docker `node:24-bookworm`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated memory-wiki test vault under `/tmp`

### Steps

1. Create an isolated memory-wiki vault with a lintable synthesis page.
2. Invoke `createWikiLintTool(config).execute(...)`.
3. Inspect the visible text and `details.reportPath`.

### Expected

- Tool text uses `Report: reports/lint.md` for vault-internal reports.
- Tool `details.reportPath` is `reports/lint.md` for vault-internal reports.
- The underlying CLI/linter behavior remains unchanged.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm test:extension memory-wiki` passed in Docker (`23` files, `118` tests).
  - Direct `createWikiLintTool(...).execute(...)` output now uses `reports/lint.md` and omits `vaultRoot` from tool details.
- Edge cases checked:
  - The underlying CLI/linter result shape was left unchanged so existing `lint.test.ts` absolute-path file reads continue to work.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - I did not reproduce the exact full-runtime `unable to resolve opened file path` failure in Docker.

Codex review:

- Ran `codex review --base origin/main` locally as requested by the contribution guide.
- It produced one P1 finding about `scripts/e2e/telegram-user-credential.ts`, but this PR does not touch that file or any credential helper path; the changed files are only `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.ts` and `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.test.ts`.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: tool consumers that expected an absolute `details.reportPath` may now see a relative path and no longer receive `vaultRoot` in the agent-tool details payload.
  - Mitigation: this is limited to the agent tool wrapper; the CLI/linter result remains unchanged for file-oriented callers, and agent-tool consumers should resolve `reports/lint.md` relative to their existing memory-wiki vault context rather than relying on a model-visible absolute path.

target.title: fix(qqbot): shorten typing keepalive window
target.body:
## Summary

  - Problem: QQBot C2C typing used a 60s input window and renewed every 50s, so if a user left and re-
entered the chat around 10s later, the typing indicator could be absent until the next 50s renewal.
  - Why it matters: Long renewal gaps make the bot look idle while it is still processing, especially when
users switch away from and back to the conversation.
  - What changed: Shortened the QQBot typing input window to 10s and renewed it every 5s while a response
is in progress.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to group messages, reply delivery, token handling,
streaming behavior, or QQBot message payload shape beyond `input_second`.

  ## Change Type (select all)

  - [x] Bug fix
  - [ ] Feature
  - [ ] Refactor required for the fix
  - [ ] Docs
  - [ ] Security hardening
  - [ ] Chore/infra

  ## Scope (select all touched areas)

  - [x] Gateway / orchestration
  - [ ] Skills / tool execution
  - [ ] Auth / tokens
  - [ ] Memory / storage
  - [x] Integrations
  - [ ] API / contracts
  - [ ] UI / DX
  - [ ] CI/CD / infra

  ## Linked Issue/PR

  - Closes #
  - Related #
  - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

  - Behavior or issue addressed: QQBot C2C typing indicator renews sooner so users who leave and re-enter
the chat do not wait up to 50s for the next typing refresh.
  - Real environment tested: Not tested against a live QQBot account in this patch.
  - Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/
gateway/typing-keepalive.test.ts`
  - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log,
linked artifact, or copied live output): Focused unit test passed locally.
  - Observed result after fix: `TypingKeepAlive` does not renew before 5s, renews at 5s, sends `inputSecond
= 10`, and stops renewing after `stop()`.
  - What was not tested: Live QQ client UI behavior after leaving and re-entering a C2C chat.
  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing code renewed every 50s with `input_second = 60`.

  ## Root Cause (if applicable)

  - Root cause: The keepalive cadence matched a long 60s QQ input-notify window, leaving a large gap before
the next renewal.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: There was no focused test locking the QQBot typing renewal interval and
input window.
  - Contributing context (if known): Re-entering a chat can lose the visible typing state before the
existing 50s renewal fires.

  ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - Coverage level that should have caught this:
    - [x] Unit test
    - [ ] Seam / integration test
    - [ ] End-to-end test
    - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/typing-keepalive.test.ts`
  - Scenario the test should lock in: QQBot typing renews every 5s and sends a 10s `inputSecond` value.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is in the keepalive timer and payload argument,
which can be tested directly with fake timers.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None found.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  QQBot C2C typing indicators now refresh more frequently during long-running replies: 10s display window,
renewed every 5s.

  ## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```text
  Before:
  [user sends C2C message] -> [typing shown for 60s] -> [renew after 50s]

  After:
  [user sends C2C message] -> [typing shown for 10s] -> [renew every 5s while processing]
  ```

  ## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No
  - New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) Yes
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No
  - Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: The existing QQ input-notify request is sent more frequently
    while a C2C response is in progress. Scope is limited to the existing typing keepalive path and stops
    when the response completes.

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment

  - OS: Local development checkout
  - Runtime/container: Node via repo Vitest wrapper
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): QQBot C2C typing keepalive
  - Relevant config (redacted): N/A

  ### Steps

  1. Start QQBot processing for a C2C message.
  2. Keep the response in progress long enough for typing keepalive renewal.
  3. Observe the keepalive renewal cadence.

  ### Expected

  - Typing renews every 5s with input_second = 10.

  ### Actual

  - Before this patch, typing renewed every 50s with input_second = 60.

  ## Evidence

  - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
  - [ ] Trace/log snippets
  - [ ] Screenshot/recording
  - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

  ## Human Verification (required)

  What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - Verified scenarios: Focused fake-timer unit test confirms no renewal before 5s, renewal at 5s,
    inputSecond = 10, and no renewals after stop().
  - Edge cases checked: Stop cancels future keepalive sends.
  - What you did not verify: Live QQ client UI behavior.

  ## Review Conversations

  - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  ## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes
  - Config/env changes? (Yes/No) No
  - Migration needed? (Yes/No) No
  - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

  ## Risks and Mitigations

  - Risk: More frequent input-notify calls could increase QQBot API traffic during long responses.
      - Mitigation: The path is limited to C2C typing keepalive, uses the existing request shape, and stops
        when processing completes.

target.title: fix: Admin HTTP RPC can execute against another live gateway instance
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: plugin HTTP routes could call `dispatchGatewayMethod()` with a scoped client but no scoped gateway context, so `admin-http-rpc` could fall back to the process-global gateway context when multiple gateway instances lived in one Node process.
- What changed: server-local `GatewayRequestContext` values are now associated with each plugin registry and injected into plugin HTTP request/upgrade runtime scopes before route handlers run.
- Guardrail: added a regression test proving a handler created for one server keeps that server's context even when another registry is active/pinned for route resolution.
- Scope boundary: no unrelated defaults, migrations, permissions, or compatibility behavior were intentionally changed.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Security hardening

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83486

## Real Behavior Proof

- Exact command: `pnpm exec oxlint src/ && pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`
- Observed result: `passed_with_baseline_failures`
- Baseline notes: `pnpm build` and `pnpm check` passed. The full `src/` lint and full test suite still report known baseline failures; the staged comparator recorded no new lint failures and accepted the run under the repository baseline policy.
- Additional focused checks run during rework:
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server/plugins-http.ts src/gateway/server/plugins-http.runtime-scopes.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/server/plugins-http.runtime-scopes.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/gateway-method-runtime.test.ts extensions/admin-http-rpc/src/handler.test.ts`

## Root Cause

`dispatchGatewayMethodInProcessRaw()` correctly prefers `scope?.context`, but plugin HTTP routes only populated the request scope with a synthetic client. When no scoped context was present, dispatch fell back to the single process-global gateway context resolver. In a multi-gateway process, that resolver could point at the last-started gateway, so a request accepted by gateway A could execute against gateway B.

## Fix

- Added a per-plugin-registry gateway context map in `src/gateway/server-plugins.ts`.
- Recorded the current server's `GatewayRequestContext` against its plugin registry during `startGatewayServer()`.
- Propagated that same context when attached plugin runtime reload replaces the plugin registry.
- Injected the server-local context into plugin HTTP request and upgrade runtime scopes.
- Kept route-table selection separate from execution-context binding: route resolution may still use the active/pinned route registry, but the dispatch context comes from the server handler's own registry.

## Regression Test Plan

- Updated `src/gateway/server/plugins-http.runtime-scopes.test.ts`.
- The new test sets one registry as the server-local registry and another as the active/pinned route registry. It verifies the route handler observes the server-local context, not the active registry's context.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, restricted to binding in-process gateway method dispatch to the correct server instance.
- Data access scope changed? Yes, the fix prevents an authenticated caller on one gateway from dispatching into another live gateway instance in the same process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: Yes.
- Config/env changes: No.
- Migration needed: No.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: plugin HTTP route reload behavior could lose the server-local context.
- Mitigation: `replaceAttachedPluginRuntime()` reapplies the stored server context to replacement registries, and the regression test covers the active-route-registry/server-registry mismatch.

target.title: fix(minimax): normalize OAuth token expiry to absolute millisecond timestamp
target.body:
## Summary

Converts MiniMax OAuth token `expired_in` from relative seconds to an absolute millisecond timestamp, fixing `hasUsableOAuthCredential()` always treating the token as expired.

## Root Cause

MiniMax returns `expired_in` from the token endpoint as a relative duration in seconds (standard OAuth `expires_in` semantics), but the auth profile store's [`hasUsableOAuthCredential()`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/agents/auth-profiles/credential-state.ts#L42) expects an absolute millisecond timestamp:

```
// credential-state.ts: remainingMs = expires - Date.now()
// 86400 - 1716000000000 < 0 → always "expired"
```

Without conversion the token appears perpetually expired, triggering a slow OAuth refresh network call to `api.minimaxi.com` on **every request**. Combined with CN network latency this causes the reported 30-50s auth-stage delay.

## Changes

**`extensions/minimax/oauth.ts`**: Added `normalizeOAuthExpires()` that handles three possible `expired_in` formats:
- Relative duration in seconds (< 1B): `Date.now() + expiredIn * 1000`
- Absolute Unix timestamp in seconds (1B–1T): `expiredIn * 1000`
- Absolute millisecond timestamp (>= 1T): use as-is

## Verification

- [x] MiniMax extension tests: 107 pass
- [x] Auth controller tests: 7 pass
- [x] Auth profile tests: 70 pass
- [x] MiniMax contract tests: 7 pass
- [x] Build succeeds
- [x] Formatting clean

Fixes #83449.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: TTS could read emoji and symbol characters aloud even when the desired output was plain spoken text.
- Why it matters: voice output becomes noisy and unnatural (for example reading symbol names) when messages contain emoji or decorative symbols.
- What changed: added `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`, registered it in config/schema, and applied filtering in the shared TTS path before synthesis.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no default behavior change when the option is disabled, and no provider-specific voice selection or transport behavior was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77969
- Related #78172
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux with branch `pr-78172`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `git checkout pr-78172`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > strips emoji when skipEmojiSymbols is enabled
✓ src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > preserves emoji when skipEmojiSymbols is disabled
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       14 passed (14)
```
- Observed result after fix: the branch proves both sides of the toggle — emoji are stripped when enabled and preserved when disabled — and the config field is present in both type and schema registration.
- What was not tested: pronunciation differences across every speech backend/provider with large emoji-heavy inputs.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this branch there was no shared-path filtering toggle for emoji/symbol stripping.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: TTS preprocessing had no configurable shared-path filter for emoji/symbol stripping, so symbol characters went straight into synthesis.
- Missing detection / guardrail: config path/schema coverage and branch-level tests did not previously lock in the enabled/disabled behavior for emoji stripping.
- Contributing context (if known): review follow-up corrected the config path and moved the filtering to the shared TTS path so behavior is applied consistently.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: emoji are removed when `skipEmojiSymbols` is true and preserved when false.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the tool test exercises the actual TTS tool path and verifies the text sent to synthesis without requiring live provider calls.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the branch adds explicit enabled/disabled emoji tests in `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can now enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. When disabled or unset, behavior remains unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[text with emoji/symbols] -> [tts synthesis] -> [emoji/symbol names may be spoken]

After:
[text with emoji/symbols] -> [optional strip step] -> [tts synthesis] -> [cleaner spoken output]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): TTS tool path
- Relevant config (redacted): `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false` in test scenarios

### Steps

1. Check out `pr-78172`.
2. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
3. Verify both emoji-enabled and emoji-disabled scenarios pass.

### Expected

- Emoji are stripped when enabled.
- Emoji are preserved when disabled.

### Actual

- Passed: `Test Files 1 passed`, `Tests 14 passed`, including both new toggle-specific scenarios.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: config/schema wiring and the two toggle-specific TTS tool tests.
- Edge cases checked: existing TTS tool tests still pass alongside the new emoji-strip tests.
- What you did **not** verify: provider-specific live voice output across all vendors/channels.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required if left unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping regex could remove characters users intended to keep.
  - Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and covered by enabled/disabled tests.
- Risk: inconsistent filtering between call paths.
  - Mitigation: filter is applied in the shared TTS path and schema-registered for consistent runtime behavior.

---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > passes the active agent id to speech generation 1ms
 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > passes the active account id to speech generation 1ms
 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > echoes longer utterances verbatim into the tool-result content 1ms
 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > defuses reply-directive tokens embedded in the spoken text 1ms
 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > defuses MEDIA lines with non-ASCII leading whitespace 1ms
 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > defuses fenced-code delimiters embedded in the spoken text 1ms
 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > throws when synthesis fails so the agent records a tool error 2ms
 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > strips emoji when skipEmojiSymbols is enabled 0ms
 ✓  agents-tools  src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts > createTtsTool > preserves emoji when skipEmojiSymbols is disabled 0ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  14 passed (14)
    Start at  22:44:20
    Duration  1.32s
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `skipEmojiSymbols` was resolved without `channelId` so channel-level config overrides were silently ignored; a redundant tool-local prefilter also ran before shared config resolution, violating TTS config precedence.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
**After-fix evidence:** Terminal output above — all 14 tests pass including both skipEmojiSymbols cases.
**Observed result after fix:** Filtering now happens only in the shared `textToSpeech` path with full channelId context; tool-local prefilter removed.
**What was not tested:** Live TTS provider invocation with a real audio output; channel override behavior verified at the unit level through the shared config resolution path.

target.title: feat(mattermost): add automatic ack reactions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Mattermost supported typing, preview streaming, and manual `message action=react`, but it did not send automatic ack reactions for accepted inbound messages.
- Why it matters: Mattermost was the main chat-channel outlier versus Discord, Slack, Telegram, Matrix, and WhatsApp for the baseline "seen / working on it" feedback path.
- What changed: added Mattermost automatic ack reactions, Mattermost emoji-name normalization for common unicode defaults, optional `channels.mattermost.ackReaction` / `channels.mattermost.accounts.<id>.ackReaction` overrides, and the matching generated config-surface artifacts.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no Mattermost lifecycle status reactions, no changes to typing behavior, and no changes to draft preview streaming.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: automatic Mattermost ack reactions for accepted inbound messages, including config override resolution and optional ack cleanup after reply.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw maintainer worktree on Linux with Mattermost-focused tests, build, generated-config checks, the full changed-scope gate, and a live Mattermost workspace smoke on this exact branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reactions.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/ack-reactions.test.ts`
  - `pnpm build`
  - `pnpm check:bundled-channel-config-metadata`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  20 passed (20)

pnpm build -> passed
pnpm check:bundled-channel-config-metadata -> passed
pnpm config:docs:check -> OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256
pnpm check:changed -> passed

Live Mattermost smoke on this branch -> passed
- started the branch with `pnpm gateway:watch`
- configured `messages.ackReactionScope=all` and `channels.mattermost.ackReaction=eyes`
- sent real Mattermost messages in the connected workspace
- observed visible ack reactions in Mattermost
- no ack/reaction failure strings appeared in the runtime logs
```
- Observed result after fix: Mattermost now has an automatic ack reaction path wired into inbound monitoring, common unicode ack emoji are normalized to Mattermost emoji names, config/docs/generated surfaces are aligned, and visible ack reactions were confirmed in a live Mattermost workspace on this exact branch.
- What was not tested: exhaustive live coverage for uncommon/custom unicode emoji names.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this patch, Mattermost only supported manual `message action=react`; there was no automatic inbound ack reaction path in the monitor.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A. This is a scoped feature/parity improvement, not a regression fix.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the first implementation pass missed the generated bundled channel metadata and config baseline hash that need to move with a public config-surface change.
- Contributing context (if known): Mattermost has a preview-streaming-first design, so this change intentionally stops at the ack layer instead of adding full status-reaction lifecycle UI.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/ack-reactions.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: account/channel/global ack resolution, mention-scope gating, emoji normalization, and `removeAckAfterReply` cleanup behavior.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the existing Mattermost websocket monitor harness hangs in this worktree, so the smallest stable coverage is the new ack helper unit coverage plus generated-surface/build/changed-gate proof.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reactions.test.ts` now covers emoji normalization through the reaction transport.
- If no new test is added, why not: a monitor-level websocket assertion was attempted and dropped because the existing harness hangs; I kept stable unit coverage rather than land a flaky test.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Mattermost can now add automatic ack reactions for accepted inbound messages.
- `messages.removeAckAfterReply` now applies to Mattermost ack reactions too.
- Mattermost now supports optional `channels.mattermost.ackReaction` and `channels.mattermost.accounts.<id>.ackReaction` overrides.
- Common unicode ack emoji like `👀` are normalized to Mattermost emoji names automatically.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[inbound Mattermost message] -> [mention/access gating] -> [typing + preview streaming] -> [reply]

After:
[inbound Mattermost message] -> [mention/access gating] -> [auto ack reaction] -> [typing + preview streaming] -> [reply] -> [optional ack removal]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - The new network calls are the existing Mattermost reaction add/remove API calls, sent only for accepted inbound messages when ack reactions are enabled. They are gated by normal Mattermost access checks, `messages.ackReaction*`, and `channels.mattermost.actions.reactions`.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node 24 worktree
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Mattermost
- Relevant config (redacted): `messages.ackReaction`, `messages.ackReactionScope`, `messages.removeAckAfterReply`, optional `channels.mattermost.ackReaction`

### Steps

1. Configure `messages.ackReaction` and `messages.ackReactionScope` for Mattermost.
2. Send an accepted inbound Mattermost message that passes normal access/mention gating.
3. Observe ack reaction creation, then send a reply path that optionally removes the ack when `removeAckAfterReply` is enabled.

### Expected

- Mattermost adds the configured ack reaction when the inbound message qualifies.
- Common unicode defaults normalize to Mattermost emoji names.
- Ack cleanup only happens after a visible reply is delivered.

### Actual

- Verified via new unit coverage, generated-surface checks, build, `pnpm check:changed`, and a live Mattermost smoke on this exact branch that showed visible ack reactions plus live ack removal after visible replies.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: config override resolution, mention-scope gating, common emoji normalization, `removeAckAfterReply`, `actions.reactions=false`, generated config metadata, config baseline hash, build, the full changed-scope gate, live Mattermost ack creation on this exact branch, and live ack removal after visible replies.
- Edge cases checked: no ack when the message is dropped before dispatch, no ack removal when no reply is delivered, and no default-group ack for open `chatmode: "onmessage"` rooms when scope remains `group-mentions`.
- What you did **not** verify: uncommon/custom emoji names beyond the common normalized set.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - Mattermost shortcode normalization is not exhaustive for every possible unicode emoji.
  - Mitigation:
    - Common/default ack emoji are normalized explicitly, and raw shortcode config still works.
- Risk:
  - Live proof does not exhaustively cover uncommon/custom emoji names.
  - Mitigation:
    - Added focused unit coverage, ran build, refreshed generated config artifacts, passed `pnpm check:changed`, confirmed live ack creation and live cleanup after visible replies, and verified that `NO_REPLY` leaves the ack in place as expected.

target.title: fix(telegram): honor outbound media max bytes
target.body:
Telegram final replies with media above the default cap can fail even when users configured a larger `channels.telegram.mediaMaxMb` limit.

Fixes #46023

## Affected surface

- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-core.ts`
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts`
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts`

## Scope

This is a narrow parameter-threading fix. It carries the existing Telegram media byte cap into final and native reply delivery, then reuses `buildOutboundMediaLoadOptions({ maxBytes })` when outbound media is loaded. No new config, policy, or media-limit strategy is introduced.

Approximate scope: 52 inserted lines / 4 removed lines including regression tests.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram final/native replies with media now thread the configured 50 MiB `mediaMaxMb` cap into outbound media loading instead of falling back to the default cap.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux executor at `/code/workspaces/openclaw/openclaw`, branch `wolf/telegram-outbound-media-maxbytes`, head `935da229b9`, Node.js `v22.22.2`, pnpm workspace install already present.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:changed --base origin/main`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `gitleaks protect --staged --redact`
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied terminal output from the local after-fix run:

```text
$ pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  138 passed (138)

$ pnpm check:changed --base origin/main
check:changed completed changed extension lanes, extension typecheck, extension test typecheck, oxlint, media helper guard, runtime sidecar loader guard, and import cycle check with no failures.

$ git diff --check
<no output>

$ gitleaks protect --staged --redact
no leaks found
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The new Telegram media delivery regression observes `loadWebMedia("https://example.com/photo.jpg", { maxBytes: 52428800 })` for a final media reply. The dispatch and native-command regressions observe `mediaMaxBytes: 52428800` threaded into `deliverReplies` delivery options. The changed-lane check, whitespace check, and staged secret scan also completed successfully.
- **What was not tested**: Live Telegram API delivery of an oversized real file was not run from this executor because no Telegram live proof credential/environment is available here. If external API proof is required, this PR needs maintainer-side live Telegram proof or a maintainer `proof: override`.

## Coverage note

ClawSweeper confirmed in issue #46023 that current main still has this gap: Telegram computes `mediaMaxBytes`, but normal final replies and native-command replies do not thread a media cap into `deliverReplies`, `deliverMediaReply`, or `loadWebMedia`. I also checked open PR coverage before starting; no open PR covered #46023, `Telegram mediaMaxMb`, or `deliverReplies mediaMaxBytes`.

target.title: fix(auth): clean refresh contention diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep OAuth `refresh_contention` diagnostics deterministic by avoiding nested file-lock cause flattening in user-facing refresh failures.
- Preserve programmatic `code` / `lockPath` on `OAuthManagerRefreshError` while redacting lock paths from messages and JSON-facing diagnostics, including structured non-`Error` causes.
- Add regression coverage for manager-level redaction and resolver-level single-message surfacing.

## Related PRs and Issues
- Follow-up to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82670, which landed the first auth refresh redaction/pass over the agents reliability slice.
- Related to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67876 and closed https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/26322, the original cross-agent OAuth refresh serialization/race fix.
- Adjacent to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82117 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80738 for Codex OAuth refresh fallback/forced-refresh behavior.
- Adjacent to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/83312 for legacy Codex OAuth sidecar compatibility.
- Related but not closing https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/42541; that issue is already closed by the prior auth-refresh work.
- Closes: none. Supersedes: none.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.test.ts`: 4 files / 76 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.ts`: 0 warnings / 0 errors.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch` on head `d754e783bc69e4c50dd57d79bd9db31519540ea5`: no accepted/actionable findings.
- `pnpm check:changed` through Blacksmith Testbox via `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ...`: `tbx_01krwxe431qkbdqzyvhcbgggdq`, GitHub Actions https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26018020246, exit 0. The changed gate widened to all lanes because the rebased branch triggered a 546-file sync/change threshold, so this is broader than the five-file PR diff.
- PR head CI sanity: Real behavior proof succeeded on https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26018018362; merge state is clean.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OAuth refresh contention failures no longer expose file-lock paths in user-facing refresh diagnostics, and the contention text is surfaced once instead of duplicated through cause-chain flattening.

Real environment tested: Fresh OpenClaw gwt branch off current `origin/main`, with focused local auth-profile tests, focused lint, branch re-review, and Blacksmith Testbox changed-gate proof on the pushed PR head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Regression tests assert `refresh_contention` messages do not contain the synthetic lock path or `file lock timeout`, including structured non-`Error` causes, and that both `OAuth token refresh failed` and `OAuth refresh failed (refresh_contention)` appear only once. Autoreview found no actionable correctness issues, and Testbox-through-Crabbox passed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0.

Observed result after fix: Focused auth-profile tests passed, focused oxlint passed, branch review passed, and Testbox changed-gate passed on the pushed head `d754e783bc69e4c50dd57d79bd9db31519540ea5`.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): make wiki_lint tool output path-safe
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `wiki_lint` returned the raw lint result as tool `details`, including an absolute `reportPath`, while the CLI path works normally.
- Why it matters: absolute-path-heavy tool output can interact poorly with tool-result handling and produced `unable to resolve opened file path` in the reported runtime path.
- What changed: the `wiki_lint` tool now renders vault-internal report paths relative to the wiki vault with a defensive formatter, and returns a lightweight details object instead of the raw lint result object.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the underlying `lintMemoryWikiVault()` result shape and CLI behavior are unchanged; CLI callers still receive the absolute report path they can read from disk.

AI-assisted PR: yes. I used OpenClaw/Codex assistance, reviewed the diff, and personally ran the verification below.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83420
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `wiki_lint` tool output should avoid exposing the raw absolute `reportPath` in model-visible text and should not return the raw lint result object as details.
- Real environment tested:
  - Host: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic x64
  - Container: `node:24-bookworm`
  - Source: `origin/main` plus this patch
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v /tmp/openclaw-83420-main:/repo \
  -w /repo node:24-bookworm \
  bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null && pnpm test:extension memory-wiki'
```

I also ran a direct Docker harness invoking `createWikiLintTool(...).execute(...)` against an isolated test vault with lint warnings.

- Evidence after fix (copied live output):

```text
[test-extension] Running 23 test files for memory-wiki
...
Test Files  23 passed (23)
Tests       118 passed (118)
```

Direct current-head tool output after the patch (`69baeae2888b1e652740c3afbdabf08f4ae84eb3`):

```text
toolText=Issues: 3 total (0 errors, 3 warnings)\nContradictions: 0\nOpen questions: 0\nProvenance gaps: 1\nReport: reports/lint.md
detailsReportPath=reports/lint.md
detailsHasVaultRoot=false
detailsContainsVaultRoot=false
toolTextContainsVaultRoot=false
coreLintReportPathAbsolute=true
coreLintReportPathContainsVaultRoot=true
```

- Observed result after fix: the tool-visible summary reports `reports/lint.md`, `details.reportPath` is also `reports/lint.md`, and the tool details no longer include or serialize the vault root. The underlying lint core result still uses an absolute report path for file-oriented callers. If a report path is not inside the vault, the formatter leaves it unchanged instead of fabricating a misleading relative path.
- What was not tested: I did not reproduce the exact full-runtime `unable to resolve opened file path` failure path in Docker; the direct tool harness reproduced the risky output shape and confirmed the patched output shape.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the patch, the same direct harness returned `Report: /tmp/wiki-lint-repro-vault/reports/lint.md` and `details.reportPath: /tmp/wiki-lint-repro-vault/reports/lint.md`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `createWikiLintTool()` reused the raw `lintMemoryWikiVault()` result directly for both visible text and tool metadata. That raw result is appropriate for CLI/file callers but too file-path-heavy for an agent tool result.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no tool-level test ensuring `wiki_lint` normalizes report paths for tool output.
- Contributing context (if known): the CLI already logs a relative report path internally, but the returned result keeps the absolute path for CLI consumers.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `wiki_lint` returns `reports/lint.md` in visible text and `details.reportPath`, without leaking the absolute vault path in the visible summary or details payload, while preserving categorized lint details such as broken wikilinks.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the tool wrapper, not the underlying linter or CLI path.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`wiki_lint` tool responses now show the lint report path relative to the vault (`reports/lint.md`) instead of an absolute local path. CLI lint output is unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
wiki_lint tool -> raw lint result -> absolute reportPath in text/details

After:
wiki_lint tool -> normalized tool payload -> relative reportPath in text/details
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic x64
- Runtime/container: Docker `node:24-bookworm`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated memory-wiki test vault under `/tmp`

### Steps

1. Create an isolated memory-wiki vault with a lintable synthesis page.
2. Invoke `createWikiLintTool(config).execute(...)`.
3. Inspect the visible text and `details.reportPath`.

### Expected

- Tool text uses `Report: reports/lint.md` for vault-internal reports.
- Tool `details.reportPath` is `reports/lint.md` for vault-internal reports.
- The underlying CLI/linter behavior remains unchanged.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm test:extension memory-wiki` passed in Docker (`23` files, `118` tests).
  - Direct `createWikiLintTool(...).execute(...)` output now uses `reports/lint.md` and omits `vaultRoot` from tool details.
- Edge cases checked:
  - The underlying CLI/linter result shape was left unchanged so existing `lint.test.ts` absolute-path file reads continue to work.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - I did not reproduce the exact full-runtime `unable to resolve opened file path` failure in Docker.

Codex review:

- Ran `codex review --base origin/main` locally as requested by the contribution guide.
- It produced one P1 finding about `scripts/e2e/telegram-user-credential.ts`, but this PR does not touch that file or any credential helper path; the changed files are only `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.ts` and `extensions/memory-wiki/src/tool.test.ts`.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: tool consumers that expected an absolute `details.reportPath` may now see a relative path and no longer receive `vaultRoot` in the agent-tool details payload.
  - Mitigation: this is limited to the agent tool wrapper; the CLI/linter result remains unchanged for file-oriented callers, and agent-tool consumers should resolve `reports/lint.md` relative to their existing memory-wiki vault context rather than relying on a model-visible absolute path.

target.title: fix(exec): return approved WebChat gateway exec output inline
target.body:
## Summary

Return approved WebChat gateway exec output as the foreground `exec` tool result.

When an exec command originates from WebChat and the gateway can surface native approval, the exec tool now waits for that approval decision inline. If the user approves, the command continues through the normal foreground exec path and the model receives the real command output in the same tool call. If the user denies, the denial is returned as a failed foreground exec result.

Other channels keep the existing async approval follow-up behavior.

## Problem

The current WebChat gateway approval path can return `approval-pending` to the model before the command output is available. That works for asynchronous notification-style channels, but it is problematic for WebChat agent turns where the model is expected to inspect command output and continue the same response.

For example, if the user asks WebChat to run `whoami` and report the output, the original model turn should receive `openclaw` as the exec result after approval. Returning only `approval-pending` can cause the model to summarize incomplete state, retry commands, or expose internal approval text instead of reasoning over the real output.

## Approach

- Detect WebChat/internal message-channel exec turns in the gateway approval path.
- Await the gateway approval decision inline for that native WebChat path.
- On approval, continue through foreground exec so the actual command output becomes the tool result.
- On denial, return a foreground failed exec result.
- Preserve the async follow-up path for non-WebChat / non-inline approval cases.

## Related Work

This is related to, but separate from:

- #77672, which improves WebChat approval routing through the backend command path.
- #56581, which preserves WebChat async approval follow-up behavior.
- #78184, which cleans up approval-pending prompts.
- #58479, which tracks approval consumption problems.

This PR focuses specifically on foreground tool-result semantics for WebChat agent mode: after native approval, the model should receive the actual command output in the same exec tool call.

## Tests

All passed locally with Node 24:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/commands-diagnostics.test.ts

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts

pnpm tsgo:core:test

git diff --check
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: WebChat exec approval should not leave the model with only an `approval-pending` result. After the user approves a native WebChat exec request, the same agent turn should receive the real command output and continue the response.
- **Real environment tested**: Downstream WebChat canary deployment using the OpenClaw patch this PR was ported from: `OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486)`, served through a WebChat UI against a gateway-hosted OpenClaw workspace.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Opened the WebChat canary environment.
  2. Sent: `Run this command and tell me the output: whoami`.
  3. Clicked the native WebChat approval button: `allow once`.
  4. Repeated with grouped read-only commands such as `pwd`, `df -h`, and `whoami` to verify the approved command output is delivered back into the same WebChat response.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live output from the WebChat canary after native approval:

  ```text
  User: Run this command and tell me the output: whoami
  Native approval: allow once
  Exec output observed by the response:
  openclaw
  ```

  Copied live output from grouped command validation:

  ```text
  User: Run these commands and summarize the output:
  pwd
  df -h
  whoami

  Native approval: allow once
  Response included copied command output ending with:
  /home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace
  ... filesystem usage from df -h ...
  openclaw
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: After approval, the WebChat response continued and summarized the actual command output instead of exposing `approval-pending`, retrying the exec command, or requiring a separate follow-up run.
- **What was not tested**: The canary validation covered WebChat/native approval behavior. Other channels were covered by existing and updated unit tests to ensure they keep the async follow-up path.

target.title: Scope agent prompt surfaces by runtime
target.body:
## Summary

This PR separates runtime-specific prompt fragments by prompt surface so PI, CLI, subagent, and native Codex app-server prompts do not inherit each other's assumptions.

- Adds a small prompt-surface helper and public plugin command guidance types for scoped agent prompt guidance.
- Keeps legacy `agentPromptGuidance: string[]` behavior while allowing structured `{ text, surfaces }` entries.
- Scopes Codex native command guidance to the main PI/OpenClaw agent prompt surface instead of subagent or CLI prompt lanes.
- Applies the same surface-scoped command guidance when compacted embedded runs rebuild their system prompt.
- Keeps PI's empty-tool fallback intact, while CLI backend prompts no longer mention PI or OpenClaw tool workflow hints when no OpenClaw tools are injected.
- Makes Codex app-server developer reply instructions conditional on `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, so automatic visible replies are not told to use `message` unconditionally.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: prompt routing now follows the selected runtime prompt surface instead of leaking PI/Codex/CLI/subagent assumptions through shared strings.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`, branch `codex/prompt-surface-runtime-separation`, using the repo's TypeScript runtime loader (`node --import tsx`) against the actual prompt builders.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pwd
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { buildAgentSystemPrompt } from './src/agents/system-prompt.ts';
import { buildCliAgentSystemPrompt } from './src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.ts';
import { resolveAgentPromptSurfaceForSessionKey } from './src/agents/prompt-surface.ts';
import { buildDeveloperInstructions } from './extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts';
import { clearPluginCommands, registerPluginCommand } from './src/plugins/commands.ts';

const workspaceDir = process.cwd();
clearPluginCommands();
registerPluginCommand('probe-plugin', {
  name: 'probe_cmd',
  description: 'Probe command',
  agentPromptGuidance: [
    { text: 'PI main probe guidance.', surfaces: ['pi_main'] },
    { text: 'CLI backend probe guidance.', surfaces: ['cli_backend'] },
    { text: 'Codex app-server probe guidance.', surfaces: ['codex_app_server'] },
    { text: 'ACP backend probe guidance.', surfaces: ['acp_backend'] },
    { text: 'Subagent probe guidance.', surfaces: ['subagent'] },
  ],
  handler: async () => ({ text: 'ok' }),
});
const piPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: 'pi_main', nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['PI main probe guidance.'] });
const cliPrompt = buildCliAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, tools: [], modelDisplay: 'test/model' });
const acpSurface = resolveAgentPromptSurfaceForSessionKey('agent:codex:acp:probe');
const acpPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: acpSurface, nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['ACP backend probe guidance.'] });
const subagentPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: 'subagent', nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['Subagent probe guidance.'] });
const baseCodexParams = {
  agentDir: workspaceDir,
  workspaceDir,
  provider: 'openai-codex-responses',
  modelId: 'gpt-5.4-codex',
  config: {},
  prompt: 'probe prompt',
};
const messageToolOnlyDeveloper = buildDeveloperInstructions({
  ...baseCodexParams,
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: 'message_tool_only',
});
const automaticDeveloper = buildDeveloperInstructions({
  ...baseCodexParams,
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: 'automatic',
});

const probe = {
  cwd: workspaceDir,
  piHasPiFallback: piPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  cliHasPiFallback: cliPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  cliHasBackendFallback: cliPrompt.includes('No OpenClaw tool list is injected for this runtime prompt surface'),
  cliHasLongWaitHint: cliPrompt.includes('For long waits'),
  cliHasSessionsSpawnHint: cliPrompt.includes('Larger work: use `sessions_spawn`'),
  cliHasPollingHint: cliPrompt.includes('Do not poll `subagents list` / `sessions_list` in a loop'),
  cliHasCliScopedGuidance: cliPrompt.includes('CLI backend probe guidance.'),
  cliHasPiScopedGuidance: cliPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  acpSurfaceFromSessionKey: acpSurface,
  acpHasAcpScopedGuidance: acpPrompt.includes('ACP backend probe guidance.'),
  acpHasPiScopedGuidance: acpPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  acpHasPiFallback: acpPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  subagentHasSubagentGuidance: subagentPrompt.includes('Subagent probe guidance.'),
  subagentHasPiGuidance: subagentPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  messageToolOnlySaysUseMessage: messageToolOnlyDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies: use `message`'),
  automaticSaysUseMessage: automaticDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies: use `message`'),
  automaticUsesActiveDeliveryPath: automaticDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies should use the active Codex delivery path'),
  codexHasCodexScopedGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('Codex app-server probe guidance.'),
  codexHasPiScopedGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  codexHasNativeSpawnAgentGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('Use Codex native `spawn_agent` for Codex subagents'),
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(probe, null, 2));
clearPluginCommands();
EOF
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the live prompt probe:

```text
/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation
{
  "cwd": "/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation",
  "piHasPiFallback": true,
  "cliHasPiFallback": false,
  "cliHasBackendFallback": true,
  "cliHasLongWaitHint": false,
  "cliHasSessionsSpawnHint": false,
  "cliHasPollingHint": false,
  "cliHasCliScopedGuidance": true,
  "cliHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "acpSurfaceFromSessionKey": "acp_backend",
  "acpHasAcpScopedGuidance": true,
  "acpHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "acpHasPiFallback": false,
  "subagentHasSubagentGuidance": true,
  "subagentHasPiGuidance": false,
  "messageToolOnlySaysUseMessage": true,
  "automaticSaysUseMessage": false,
  "automaticUsesActiveDeliveryPath": true,
  "codexHasCodexScopedGuidance": true,
  "codexHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "codexHasNativeSpawnAgentGuidance": true
}
```
